images.caption:
Figure 1:Overview of EgoCS-400K.EgoCS-400K is a large-scale multi-grained egocentric Counter-Strike dataset built from first-person gameplay videos across CS:GO and CS2 maps, covering 10,000+ hours, 40,000+ rounds, 1,000+ matches, 13 maps, and 10 player viewpoints.
The dataset provides a hierarchical organization from player-view sequences to segments, protected action chains, protected atomic actions, and per-tick state traces.
For each segment, EgoCS-400K further provides rich replay-grounded annotations, including segment-level and protected-segment captions, action sequences, camera and movement descriptions, prompts, keyboard/mouse signals, and per-tick states.
This multi-level design enables fine-grained analysis of embodied actions, temporal dynamics, and player-environment interactions in complex competitive scenarios.

Figure 2:Construction pipeline of EgoCS-400K.Starting from public Counter-Strike demos, we first collect raw replay files, render synchronized first-person videos, and filter invalid rendered videos.
We then parse each demo into player-level per-tick data.
From the parsed action and spatial signals, we construct keyboard/mouse annotations, atomic action sequences, protected action chains, and video segments.
Finally, we construct prior-guided VLM prompts to generate structured segment-level and protected-chain-level captions.
Figure 3:A four-second qualitative example. The visualization aligns first-person frames, input traces, action annotations, and the generated prompt within the same segment.
